paper: arXiv:2412.16177 (Chuharski, Rojas Collins, Meringolo, 'Mining Math Conjectures from LLMs: A Pruning Approach', v1, 9 Dec 2024)
statement_verbatim: Let G be a non-solvable group. For any element x in G, if SolG(x) is a proper subgroup of G, then the intersection of SolG(x) with its normalizer in G is always metabelian.
statement_context: Appendix A.5.6 'Additional Conjectures': 'The following conjectures are just a couple of the conjectures that had code that was unable to be run but are still potentially interesting from Claude' — i.e. the conjecture was never machine-validated by the authors.

counterexample.group: G = A5 x S4, realized as a permutation group on 9 points: A5 acts on {0,1,2,3,4}, S4 acts on {5,6,7,8}. |G| = 1440.

claimed_sol_description: Sol_G(x) = D10 x S4 where D10 = <(01234),(14)(23)> is dihedral of order 10
conclusion: Hypothesis of A.16 holds (Sol_G(x) is a proper subgroup of the non-solvable G) but the conclusion fails: Sol ∩ N_G(Sol) = D10 x S4 has second derived subgroup 1 x V4 != 1, hence is not metabelian. Conjecture A.16 is REFUTED.
